code: @Manual{,   title = {hfsubset: Lightweight subsetter for hydrofabric files},   author = {Mike Johnson},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.2},   url = {https://github.com/lynker-spatial/hfsubset}, }

title: Lightweight subsetter for hydrofabric files
text: hfsubset provides lightweight, dependency-minimal subsetting utility working Lynker Spatial Hydrofabrics. designed quickly extract spatial network subsets large hydrologic datasets, returning upstream contributing features relevant specified location.

title: Lightweight subsetter for hydrofabric files
text: function operates Hydrofabric GeoPackage—standardized container vector-based hydrologic data layers (e.g., flowpaths, catchments, waterbodies). Given reference point NHDPlusV2 COMID (comid), Hydrofabric ID (id), hydrolocation reference (hl_reference), identifies extracts upstream features connected hydrologic network. output layer returned sf object containing features contributing specified reference location. structure makes straightforward visualize, analyze, export targeted drainage areas without needing process full dataset. optional outfile parameter allows writing subsetted layers back new GeoPackage (OGR spec single layer calls) persistent storage sharing, , left NULL, allows data returned memory.

title: Lightweight subsetter for hydrofabric files
text: convention allows hfsubset resolve references across multiple hydrologic networks catalog systems without ambiguity.
code: -   **src**: Path to a local Hydrofabric GeoPackage/geoparquet (typically containing layers such as flowpaths, catchments, waterbodies, etc.) >   **reference**: A hydrologic location identifier. This can be either: -   `id`: A Hydrofabric ID -   `comid`: An NHDPlusV2 COMID -   `hl_reference`: A Hydrolocation reference (`hl_reference`). A hydrolocation reference (`hl_reference`) is a standardized string that encodes both the data source and the native identifier in the form: `{source}-{native_id}`

title: Lightweight subsetter for hydrofabric files
code: library(hfsubset) library(mapview)  x <- hfsubset(src =  glue::glue('{base_dir}/v3.0/reference_fabric.gpkg'),               hl_reference = "nwis-07187000") #> ℹ Inferred store type: ogr_store #> ℹ Origin flowpath: 7590701 (VPU: 11) lobstr::tree(x, max_depth = 1) #> <list> #> ├─flowpaths: S3<sf/tbl_df/tbl/data.frame>... #> ├─divides: S3<sf/tbl_df/tbl/data.frame>... #> ├─network: S3<tbl_df/tbl/data.frame>... #> ├─hydrolocations: S3<sf/tbl_df/tbl/data.frame>... #> └─pois: S3<sf/tbl_df/tbl/data.frame>... hfview(x) #> Google Chrome was not found. Try setting the `CHROMOTE_CHROME` environment variable to the executable of a Chromium-based browser, such as Google Chrome, Chromium or Brave. y <- hfsubset(src =  glue::glue('{base_dir}/v3.0/refactored_fabric.gpkg'),               hl_reference = "nwis-07187000")  #> ℹ Inferred store type: ogr_store #> ℹ Origin flowpath: 7590701 (VPU: 11) lobstr::tree(y, max_depth = 1) #> <list> #> ├─flowpaths: S3<sf/tbl_df/tbl/data.frame>... #> ├─divides: S3<sf/tbl_df/tbl/data.frame>... #> ├─network: S3<tbl_df/tbl/data.frame>... #> ├─hydrolocations: S3<sf/tbl_df/tbl/data.frame>... #> └─pois: S3<sf/tbl_df/tbl/data.frame>... hfview(y) z <- hfsubset(src =  glue::glue('{base_dir}/CONUS/v2.2/nextgen/v2.2_conus.gpkg'),               hl_reference = "nwis-07187000") #> ℹ Inferred store type: ogr_store #> ℹ Origin flowpath: 7590701 (VPU: 11) lobstr::tree(z, max_depth = 1) #> <list> #> ├─flowpaths: S3<sf/tbl_df/tbl/data.frame>... #> ├─divides: S3<sf/tbl_df/tbl/data.frame>... #> ├─nexus: S3<sf/tbl_df/tbl/data.frame>... #> ├─network: S3<tbl_df/tbl/data.frame>... #> ├─hydrolocations: S3<tbl_df/tbl/data.frame>... #> ├─pois: S3<tbl_df/tbl/data.frame>... #> ├─flowpath-attributes: S3<tbl_df/tbl/data.frame>... #> └─divide-attributes: S3<tbl_df/tbl/data.frame>... hfview(z) # hfutils::lynker_spatial_auth() # hfsubset(src = "ls://3.0/superconus/reference", #          hl_reference = "nwis-07187000", #          lyrs = c("network", "flowpaths", "divides")) |> #   lobstr::tree(max_depth = 1)

title: Subset a NextGen HydroFabric from either a GPKG or layered Hive-partitioned GeoParquets — hfsubset
code: hfsubset(   src,   id,   comid,   hl_reference,   gage,   xy,   outfile = NULL,   lyrs = c("flowpaths", "flowlines", "divides", "nexus", "network", "hydrolocations",     "pois", "flowpath-attributes", "divide-attributes"),   crs = NULL,   verbose = FALSE,   check = FALSE,   cache = TRUE,   optimize = TRUE )

title: Subset a NextGen HydroFabric from either a GPKG or layered Hive-partitioned GeoParquets — hfsubset
text: src Path GeoPackage (.gpkg) root directory layered hive-partitioned GeoParquet (e.g., //v1/vpu=01/part-*.parquet). store type (GeoPackage vs GeoParquet) inferred src. id Origin flowpath_id (character). comid Origin NHDPlusV2 COMID. hl_reference Origin hl_reference tag (e.g. nwis-09112500). gage Origin USGS gage id (e.g. "09112500"). Convenience hl_reference = paste0("nwis-", gage); 8-digit id expected. xy Origin point c(x, y) (defaults lon/lat, EPSG:4326). Resolved containing divide, flowpath. Uses GPKG R-tree OGR stores; reads full divides layer otherwise. outfile Optional path write subset new GeoPackage. lyrs Character vector layer names extract. Defaults c("flowpaths","divides","nexus","network","hydrolocations","pois", "flowpath-attributes","divide-attributes"). crs Optional EPSG/int sf outputs reading GeoParquet (recommended CRS missing). verbose Logical. Emit progress messages. Default FALSE. check Logical. TRUE, run hfutils::hf_check_invariants("ngen", ...) returned subset (warnings , stop). Default FALSE. cache Logical. Reuse -memory per-VPU network graph across calls (big win batch subsetting). Default TRUE. See hfsubset_clear_cache(). optimize Logical. writing outfile, index optimize GeoPackage via optimize_gpkg(). Default TRUE.

title: Subset a NextGen HydroFabric from either a GPKG or layered Hive-partitioned GeoParquets — hfsubset
text: named list tibbles/sf outfile missing; invisibly list writing otherwise.

title: Index and optimize a hydrofabric GeoPackage — optimize_gpkg
text: Make written subset cheap query re-subset. Three things, borrowed verify_indices() / R-tree handling CIROH's NGIAB_data_preprocess:

title: Index and optimize a hydrofabric GeoPackage — optimize_gpkg
Index and optimize a hydrofabric GeoPackage — optimize_gpkg
text: gpkg Path GeoPackage written hfsubset(). extra_cols Additional column names index present. verbose Logical; report indices created R-trees rebuilt.

title: Index and optimize a hydrofabric GeoPackage — optimize_gpkg
text: Attribute indices id / foreign-key columns every table (e.g. flowpath_id, divide_id, vpuid). sf writes spatial R-tree leaves attribute tables (network, *-attributes) non-geometry id columns unindexed, flowpath_id (...) re-subsetting scans whole table without . Spatial R-tree verification. GDAL builds rtree_<layer>_<geom> write time (SPATIAL_INDEX=YES). confirm feature layer one rebuild missing, index present freshly built (optimal) anything copies downstream. ANALYZE + PRAGMA optimize SQLite's planner actually uses new indices R-tree statistics current.

title: Locate the divide containing a point — store_locate_point
text: Resolve xy coordinate divide (catchment) polygon contains . Used hfsubset() support point-based origins ("subset clicked"). Mirrors lat/lon lookup CIROH's NGIAB_data_preprocess: transform point layer CRS, hit spatial index candidates, test exact containment.

what: Resolve the upstream id table for an origin flowpath — store_upstream_ids
title: Resolve the upstream id table for an origin flowpath — store_upstream_ids
text: Return subset network's id table (one row per upstream flowline/flowpath) drains origin_fp_id. result carries whichever flowpath_id, flowpath_toid, poi_id, divide_id, flowline_id schema provides – enough filter every downstream layer.

title: Resolve the upstream id table for an origin flowpath — store_upstream_ids
text: store store object. origin_fp_id Origin flowpath_id. vpu Origin VPU id (character) NA. has_vpuid Logical; whether network carries vpuid column. cache Logical; reuse per-(source, VPU) graph (default path).

title: Resolve the upstream id table for an origin flowpath — store_upstream_ids
text: data.frame upstream id columns.

title: Resolve the upstream id table for an origin flowpath — store_upstream_ids
text: Dispatch picks cheapest correct traversal backend: ogr_store pushes walk SQLite recursive CTE, touching upstream rows (whole-VPU materialization, graph build). default collects VPU's edges walks -memory igraph, caching per (source, VPU) batch reuse.

title: hfsubset 0.2
text: Subsetting works GeoPackage (OGR) GeoParquet stores, upstream network traversal origin id, comid, hl_reference. Optional check = TRUE runs hfutils::hf_check_invariants("ngen", ...) returned subset (warnings ; stop). Lynker Spatial pkgdown site cross-stack navigation. Removed bundled ref_net dataset (~6 MB). vestige pre-store architecture longer consumed: origin resolution upstream traversal now run OGR/Arrow/Lynker Spatial stores, planned per-VPU sidecar (see R/sidecar.R) supersedes . Dropping also keeps Apache-2.0 code separate ODbL-tracked fabric data.
